question.en: Diagnostic question: in KMP, what information does the failure function provide?
question.ro: ГҺntrebare diagnosticДғ: Г®n KMP, ce informaИӣii oferДғ funcИӣia de eИҷec?

text.en: How many characters to skip in the text after a mismatch
text.ro: CГўte caractere sДғ sarДғ Г®n text dupДғ un mismatch

text.en: The length of the longest proper border of each prefix of the pattern
text.ro: Lungimea celui mai lung border propriu al fiecДғrui prefix al pattern-ului

text.en: The position of the next potential match in the text
text.ro: PoziИӣia urmДғtoarei potriviri potenИӣiale Г®n text

explanation.en: F[j] gives the longest proper prefix of P[0..j-1] that is also a suffix, telling KMP where to resume matching in the pattern. It doesn't directly skip text characters or point to text positions. This lecture builds on KMP to introduce Boyer-Moore (which can skip text characters) and Rabin-Karp (which uses hashing for a different approach entirely).
explanation.ro: F[j] dДғ cel mai lung prefix propriu al P[0..j-1] care este Иҷi sufix, spunГўnd KMP-ului unde sДғ reia potrivirea Г®n pattern. Nu sare direct caractere din text Иҷi nu indicДғ poziИӣii din text. Acest curs construieИҷte pe KMP pentru a introduce Boyer-Moore (care poate sДғri caractere din text) Иҷi Rabin-Karp (care foloseИҷte hashing pentru o abordare complet diferitДғ).

content.en:
**Boyer-Moore** is one of the most efficient string matching algorithms in practice. It is implemented in most text editors (Find/Replace) and in tools like `grep`.

The key insight: compare the pattern against the text **right-to-left** (unlike naive and KMP which go left-to-right). When a mismatch occurs, use two rules to compute how far to shift the pattern вҖ” potentially skipping large portions of the text. This makes Boyer-Moore **sublinear** in practice.
content.ro:
**Boyer-Moore** este unul dintre cei mai eficienИӣi algoritmi de potrivire a Иҷirurilor Г®n practicДғ. Este implementat Г®n majoritatea editoarelor de text (Find/Replace) Иҷi Г®n instrumente precum `grep`.

Ideea cheie: comparДғ pattern-ul cu textul **de la dreapta la stГўnga** (spre deosebire de naiv Иҷi KMP care merg de la stГўnga la dreapta). CГўnd apare un mismatch, foloseИҷte douДғ reguli pentru a calcula cГўt de departe sДғ deplaseze pattern-ul вҖ” potenИӣial sДғrind porИӣiuni mari din text. Aceasta face Boyer-Moore **subliniar** Г®n practicДғ.

content.en:
When a mismatch occurs, BM computes two possible shifts and chooses the **larger** one:

1. **Bad character rule**: based on the mismatched character in the text
2. **Good suffix rule**: based on the part of the pattern that DID match
content.ro:
CГўnd apare un mismatch, BM calculeazДғ douДғ deplasДғri posibile Иҷi o alege pe cea **mai mare**:

1. **Regula caracterului rДғu**: bazatДғ pe caracterul nepotrivit din text
2. **Regula sufixului bun**: bazatДғ pe partea din pattern care S-A potrivit

question.en: Why might right-to-left comparison lead to faster matching than left-to-right? Think about what happens when the last character of the pattern doesn't appear in the text at all.
question.ro: De ce comparaИӣia de la dreapta la stГўnga ar putea duce la o potrivire mai rapidДғ decГўt de la stГўnga la dreapta? GГўndiИӣi-vДғ ce se Г®ntГўmplДғ cГўnd ultimul caracter al pattern-ului nu apare deloc Г®n text.

answer.en: If the rightmost character of the pattern mismatches a text character that doesn't appear anywhere in the pattern, we can shift the entire pattern past that position in one step вҖ” skipping m characters at once! Left-to-right comparison (naive/KMP) can only shift by 1 position at minimum in this case, making right-to-left dramatically faster for large alphabets.
answer.ro: DacДғ cel mai din dreapta caracter al pattern-ului nu se potriveИҷte cu un caracter din text care nu apare nicДғieri Г®n pattern, putem deplasa Г®ntregul pattern dincolo de acea poziИӣie Г®ntr-un singur pas вҖ” sДғrind m caractere deodatДғ! ComparaИӣia de la stГўnga la dreapta (naiv/KMP) poate deplasa doar cu 1 poziИӣie minim Г®n acest caz, fДғcГўnd comparaИӣia de la dreapta la stГўnga dramatic mai rapidДғ pentru alfabete mari.

content.en:
When a mismatch occurs at position k in the pattern (comparing right-to-left), the **bad character** is `T[i+k]` вҖ” the text character that caused the mismatch.

The bad character rule determines how far to shift the pattern based on where (or whether) this character appears in the pattern.
content.ro:
CГўnd apare un mismatch la poziИӣia k Г®n pattern (comparГўnd de la dreapta la stГўnga), **caracterul rДғu** este `T[i+k]` вҖ” caracterul din text care a cauzat mismatch-ul.

Regula caracterului rДғu determinДғ cГўt de departe sДғ deplaseze pattern-ul bazГўndu-se pe unde (sau dacДғ) acest caracter apare Г®n pattern.

content.en:
**Case 1 (typical): Bad character exists in pattern to the LEFT**

Shift the pattern right so that the rightmost occurrence of the bad character in `P[0..k-1]` aligns with `T[i+k]`.

Shift amount = `k - last_occurrence_index`
content.ro:
**Cazul 1 (tipic): Caracterul rДғu existДғ Г®n pattern la STГӮNGA**

DeplaseazДғ pattern-ul la dreapta astfel Г®ncГўt cea mai din dreapta apariИӣie a caracterului rДғu Г®n `P[0..k-1]` sДғ se alinieze cu `T[i+k]`.

Deplasare = `k - index_ultima_apariИӣie`

content.en:
**Case 2: Bad character does NOT exist in pattern**

Shift the entire pattern past the bad character position.

Shift = `k + 1`
content.ro:
**Cazul 2: Caracterul rДғu NU existДғ Г®n pattern**

DeplaseazДғ Г®ntregul pattern dincolo de poziИӣia caracterului rДғu.

Deplasare = `k + 1`

content.en:
**Case 3 (negative shift): Bad character is to the RIGHT**

If the rightmost occurrence of the bad character in the pattern is to the right of position k, the bad character rule gives a negative shift. In this case, use shift = 1 (or rely on the good suffix rule for a better shift).
content.ro:
**Cazul 3 (deplasare negativДғ): Caracterul rДғu este la DREAPTA**

DacДғ cea mai din dreapta apariИӣie a caracterului rДғu Г®n pattern este la dreapta poziИӣiei k, regula caracterului rДғu dДғ o deplasare negativДғ. ГҺn acest caz, folosiИӣi deplasare = 1 (sau bazaИӣi-vДғ pe regula sufixului bun pentru o deplasare mai bunДғ).

question.en: When does the bad character rule give the largest shifts? Think about the relationship between alphabet size and shift distance.
question.ro: CГўnd dДғ regula caracterului rДғu cele mai mari deplasДғri? GГўndiИӣi-vДғ la relaИӣia dintre dimensiunea alfabetului Иҷi distanИӣa de deplasare.

answer.en: The bad character rule gives the largest shifts when the alphabet is large (e.g., ASCII with 256 characters). With a large alphabet, the probability that the bad character doesn't appear in the pattern at all is high, triggering Case 2 (shift = k+1). For DNA sequences (alphabet = {A, C, G, T}), shifts tend to be smaller since most characters appear in the pattern.
answer.ro: Regula caracterului rДғu dДғ cele mai mari deplasДғri cГўnd alfabetul este mare (de ex., ASCII cu 256 de caractere). Cu un alfabet mare, probabilitatea ca caracterul rДғu sДғ nu aparДғ deloc Г®n pattern este ridicatДғ, declanИҷГўnd Cazul 2 (deplasare = k+1). Pentru secvenИӣe ADN (alfabet = {A, C, G, T}), deplasДғrile tind sДғ fie mai mici deoarece majoritatea caracterelor apar Г®n pattern.

content.en:
The **good suffix** is the part of the pattern that matched before the mismatch occurred вҖ” the suffix `P[k+1..m-1]`.

The good suffix rule determines how far to shift based on where this already-matched portion reappears in the pattern.
content.ro:
**Sufixul bun** este partea din pattern care s-a potrivit Г®nainte de mismatch вҖ” sufixul `P[k+1..m-1]`.

Regula sufixului bun determinДғ cГўt de departe sДғ deplaseze bazГўndu-se pe unde aceastДғ porИӣiune deja potrivitДғ reapare Г®n pattern.

content.en:
**Case 1: Good suffix reappears in pattern**

If the good suffix appears elsewhere in the pattern (preceded by a different character), shift to align that occurrence with the text. This reuses the information that those characters already matched.
content.ro:
**Cazul 1: Sufixul bun reapare Г®n pattern**

DacДғ sufixul bun apare Г®n altДғ parte a pattern-ului (precedat de un caracter diferit), deplaseazДғ pentru a alinia acea apariИӣie cu textul. Aceasta reutilizeazДғ informaИӣia cДғ acele caractere s-au potrivit deja.

content.en:
**Case 2: Only a prefix of the good suffix reappears**

If no full reoccurrence exists, look for the longest suffix of the good suffix that is also a prefix of the pattern. Shift to align that prefix.
content.ro:
**Cazul 2: Doar un prefix al sufixului bun reapare**

DacДғ nu existДғ o reapariИӣie completДғ, cДғutaИӣi cel mai lung sufix al sufixului bun care este Иҷi un prefix al pattern-ului. DeplasaИӣi pentru a alinia acel prefix.

content.en:
- **Preprocessing**: O(m + |ОЈ|) вҖ” build both the bad character table and the good suffix table
- **Worst case**: O(nВ·m) вҖ” but **O(n)** with Galil's optimization
- **Average/best case**: O(n/m) вҖ” **SUBLINEAR!** Can skip characters entirely without examining them
content.ro:
- **Preprocesare**: O(m + |ОЈ|) вҖ” construieИҷte atГўt tabela caracterului rДғu cГўt Иҷi tabela sufixului bun
- **Caz defavorabil**: O(nВ·m) вҖ” dar **O(n)** cu optimizarea lui Galil
- **Caz mediu/cel mai bun**: O(n/m) вҖ” **SUBLINIAR!** Poate sДғri caractere complet fДғrДғ a le examina

question.en: Why does Boyer-Moore take both the bad character shift and the good suffix shift, then choose the maximum? Why not just use one rule?
question.ro: De ce Boyer-Moore ia atГўt deplasarea caracterului rДғu cГўt Иҷi deplasarea sufixului bun, apoi alege maximul? De ce nu foloseИҷte doar o singurДғ regulДғ?

answer.en: Each rule captures different information: the bad character rule uses information about a character NOT in the pattern, while the good suffix rule uses information about characters that DID match. In some situations one rule gives a large shift while the other gives a small shift. Taking the maximum ensures we always get the best possible shift without missing any valid alignments вҖ” it is safe because both rules independently guarantee that any skipped positions cannot yield a match.
answer.ro: Fiecare regulДғ capteazДғ informaИӣii diferite: regula caracterului rДғu foloseИҷte informaИӣii despre un caracter care NU este Г®n pattern, Г®n timp ce regula sufixului bun foloseИҷte informaИӣii despre caracterele care S-AU potrivit. ГҺn unele situaИӣii o regulДғ dДғ o deplasare mare iar cealaltДғ una micДғ. LuГўnd maximul ne asigurДғm cДғ obИӣinem Г®ntotdeauna cea mai bunДғ deplasare posibilДғ fДғrДғ a rata alinieri valide вҖ” este sigur deoarece ambele reguli garanteazДғ independent cДғ orice poziИӣie sДғritДғ nu poate produce o potrivire.

question.en: Boyer-Moore uses two rules: bad character and good suffix. If the bad character rule suggests shifting by 3 positions and the good suffix rule suggests shifting by 5, which shift does Boyer-Moore actually use? Why?
question.ro: Boyer-Moore foloseИҷte douДғ reguli: caracterul rДғu Иҷi sufixul bun. DacДғ regula caracterului rДғu sugereazДғ deplasarea cu 3 poziИӣii iar regula sufixului bun sugereazДғ deplasarea cu 5, ce deplasare foloseИҷte de fapt Boyer-Moore? De ce?

answer.en: Boyer-Moore takes the maximum of the two shifts вҖ” so it shifts by 5. Both rules independently guarantee that no match is skipped, so taking the larger shift is always safe and maximizes the distance jumped, improving efficiency.
answer.ro: Boyer-Moore ia maximul celor douДғ deplasДғri вҖ” deci se deplaseazДғ cu 5. Ambele reguli garanteazДғ independent cДғ nu se sare peste nicio potrivire, deci luarea deplasДғrii mai mari este Г®ntotdeauna sigurДғ Иҷi maximizeazДғ distanИӣa sДғritДғ, Г®mbunДғtДғИӣind eficienИӣa.

content.en:
**Rabin-Karp** takes a fundamentally different approach: instead of comparing characters, convert the pattern and each m-length substring of text into **numbers** using a hash function. Compare numbers instead of strings.

The key optimization: use a **rolling hash** to compute the next hash from the previous one in **O(1)**, rather than recomputing from scratch each time.
content.ro:
**Rabin-Karp** adoptДғ o abordare fundamental diferitДғ: Г®n loc sДғ compare caractere, converteИҷte pattern-ul Иҷi fiecare subИҷir de lungime m din text Г®n **numere** folosind o funcИӣie hash. ComparДғ numere Г®n loc de Иҷiruri.

Optimizarea cheie: foloseИҷte un **rolling hash** pentru a calcula hash-ul urmДғtor din cel anterior Г®n **O(1)**, Г®n loc sДғ recalculeze de la zero de fiecare datДғ.

content.en:
**Hash function (Horner's rule):**

Convert a string of length m into a number by treating each character as a digit in base |ОЈ| (alphabet size):

`f(S) = S[0]В·|ОЈ|^(m-1) + S[1]В·|ОЈ|^(m-2) + ... + S[m-1]В·|ОЈ|^0`

For example, with ОЈ = {A=0, B=1, ..., Z=25}: `f("BABX") = 1В·26Ві + 0В·26ВІ + 1В·26 + 23 = 17625`
content.ro:
**FuncИӣia hash (regula lui Horner):**

ConverteИҷte un Иҷir de lungime m Г®ntr-un numДғr tratГўnd fiecare caracter ca o cifrДғ Г®n baza |ОЈ| (dimensiunea alfabetului):

`f(S) = S[0]В·|ОЈ|^(m-1) + S[1]В·|ОЈ|^(m-2) + ... + S[m-1]В·|ОЈ|^0`

De exemplu, cu ОЈ = {A=0, B=1, ..., Z=25}: `f("BABX") = 1В·26Ві + 0В·26ВІ + 1В·26 + 23 = 17625`

content.en:
The rolling hash formula computes the next window's hash from the previous one in O(1):

`f(T[i+1..i+m]) = |ОЈ| В· f(T[i..i+m-1]) - T[i] В· |ОЈ|^m + T[i+m]`

Each new hash is computed by: removing the leftmost character's contribution, shifting everything left (multiply by |ОЈ|), and adding the new rightmost character.
content.ro:
Formula rolling hash calculeazДғ hash-ul ferestrei urmДғtoare din cel anterior Г®n O(1):

`f(T[i+1..i+m]) = |ОЈ| В· f(T[i..i+m-1]) - T[i] В· |ОЈ|^m + T[i+m]`

Fiecare hash nou se calculeazДғ prin: eliminarea contribuИӣiei caracterului cel mai din stГўnga, deplasarea la stГўnga (Г®nmulИӣire cu |ОЈ|) Иҷi adДғugarea noului caracter din dreapta.

content.en: **Problem: large numbers.** For long patterns, hash values become astronomically large. Solution: work modulo a prime number q. Use `fq(S) = f(S) mod q`. This keeps all values manageable but introduces the possibility of hash collisions.
content.ro: **ProblemДғ: numere mari.** Pentru pattern-uri lungi, valorile hash devin astronomic de mari. SoluИӣie: lucrДғm modulo un numДғr prim q. Folosim `fq(S) = f(S) mod q`. Aceasta menИӣine valorile gestionabile dar introduce posibilitatea coliziunilor hash.

content.en: Since we work modulo q, different strings can have the same hash (**collision**). When hashes match, we **must** verify the actual strings character by character. A position where hashes match but strings differ is called a **false positive**.
content.ro: Deoarece lucrДғm modulo q, Иҷiruri diferite pot avea acelaИҷi hash (**coliziune**). CГўnd hash-urile se potrivesc, **trebuie** sДғ verificДғm Иҷirurile reale caracter cu caracter. O poziИӣie unde hash-urile se potrivesc dar Иҷirurile diferДғ se numeИҷte **false positive**.

content.en:
- **Preprocessing**: O(m) вҖ” compute pattern hash and first window hash
- **Worst case**: O(nВ·m) вҖ” if every position is a false positive (all hashes collide)
- **Average case**: O(n + m) вҖ” for well-chosen q, very few false positives occur
content.ro:
- **Preprocesare**: O(m) вҖ” calculeazДғ hash-ul pattern-ului Иҷi al primei ferestre
- **Caz defavorabil**: O(nВ·m) вҖ” dacДғ fiecare poziИӣie este un false positive (toate hash-urile colizioneazДғ)
- **Caz mediu**: O(n + m) вҖ” pentru q bine ales, apar foarte puИӣine false positives

content.en: **Unique advantage of Rabin-Karp:** easily generalizable from 1D strings to **2D matrices** (searching for a pattern matrix inside a larger matrix). Neither KMP nor Boyer-Moore can be extended this way. This makes Rabin-Karp the go-to algorithm for 2D pattern matching problems.
content.ro: **Avantajul unic al Rabin-Karp:** uИҷor generalizabil de la Иҷiruri 1D la **matrice 2D** (cДғutarea unei matrice pattern Г®ntr-o matrice mai mare). Nici KMP nici Boyer-Moore nu pot fi extinse astfel. Aceasta face Rabin-Karp algoritmul preferat pentru probleme de potrivire 2D a pattern-urilor.

question.en: Why can't we simply skip the character-by-character verification when hashes match? What could go wrong?
question.ro: De ce nu putem pur Иҷi simplu sДғ sДғrim verificarea caracter cu caracter cГўnd hash-urile se potrivesc? Ce ar putea merge prost?

answer.en: Because we use modular arithmetic (mod q), many different strings map to the same hash value. For example, with q = 7, both hash 15 and hash 22 give remainder 1. If we skip verification, we would report false matches вҖ” positions where the hash matches but the actual text differs from the pattern. The smaller the prime q, the more frequent these false positives become.
answer.ro: Deoarece folosim aritmeticДғ modularДғ (mod q), multe Иҷiruri diferite se mapeazДғ la aceeaИҷi valoare hash. De exemplu, cu q = 7, atГўt hash 15 cГўt Иҷi hash 22 dau restul 1. DacДғ sДғrim verificarea, am raporta potriviri false вҖ” poziИӣii unde hash-ul se potriveИҷte dar textul real diferДғ de pattern. Cu cГўt primul q este mai mic, cu atГўt aceste false positives devin mai frecvente.

content.en:
Here is a summary comparison of all four string matching algorithms we have studied. Note that Boyer-Moore has the best **average** case вҖ” it is the only one that achieves sublinear performance.

*Note: Boyer-Moore worst case becomes O(n) with Galil's optimization.*
content.ro:
IatДғ o comparaИӣie sinteticДғ a tuturor celor patru algoritmi de potrivire a Иҷirurilor pe care i-am studiat. ReИӣineИӣi cДғ Boyer-Moore are cel mai bun caz **mediu** вҖ” este singurul care atinge performanИӣДғ subliniarДғ.

*NotДғ: Cazul defavorabil al Boyer-Moore devine O(n) cu optimizarea lui Galil.*

question.en: If KMP guarantees O(n) worst case while Boyer-Moore has O(nВ·m) worst case, why is Boyer-Moore preferred in practice (e.g., in grep)?
question.ro: DacДғ KMP garanteazДғ O(n) caz defavorabil Г®n timp ce Boyer-Moore are O(nВ·m) caz defavorabil, de ce este Boyer-Moore preferat Г®n practicДғ (de ex., Г®n grep)?

answer.en: Because real-world text searching deals with large alphabets (ASCII/Unicode) and typical text, not adversarial inputs. In these conditions, Boyer-Moore's average case of O(n/m) вҖ” sublinear! вҖ” massively outperforms KMP's O(n). The worst case O(nВ·m) is extremely rare in practice and can be eliminated entirely with Galil's optimization, bringing it to O(n) while retaining the sublinear average.
answer.ro: Deoarece cДғutarea realДғ Г®n text se face cu alfabete mari (ASCII/Unicode) Иҷi text tipic, nu input-uri adversariale. ГҺn aceste condiИӣii, cazul mediu al Boyer-Moore de O(n/m) вҖ” subliniar! вҖ” depДғИҷeИҷte masiv O(n) al KMP. Cazul defavorabil O(nВ·m) este extrem de rar Г®n practicДғ Иҷi poate fi eliminat complet cu optimizarea lui Galil, aducГўndu-l la O(n) pДғstrГўnd media subliniarДғ.

question.en: Why does Boyer-Moore compare the pattern against the text from right to left?
question.ro: De ce Boyer-Moore comparДғ pattern-ul cu textul de la dreapta la stГўnga?

text.en: It makes the algorithm simpler to implement
text.ro: Face algoritmul mai simplu de implementat

text.en: It enables the bad character rule to skip large portions of text, achieving sublinear performance
text.ro: Permite regulii caracterului rДғu sДғ sarДғ porИӣiuni mari de text, atingГўnd performanИӣДғ subliniarДғ

text.en: It avoids the need for preprocessing
text.ro: EvitДғ necesitatea preprocesДғrii

text.en: It guarantees O(n) worst case complexity
text.ro: GaranteazДғ complexitate O(n) Г®n cazul defavorabil

explanation.en: Right-to-left comparison allows the bad character rule to produce large shifts when the mismatched character doesn't appear in the pattern. If the last character mismatches a character absent from the pattern, the entire pattern shifts past that position вҖ” this is what yields O(n/m) average performance.
explanation.ro: ComparaИӣia de la dreapta la stГўnga permite regulii caracterului rДғu sДғ producДғ deplasДғri mari cГўnd caracterul nepotrivit nu apare Г®n pattern. DacДғ ultimul caracter nu se potriveИҷte cu un caracter absent din pattern, Г®ntregul pattern se deplaseazДғ dincolo de acea poziИӣie вҖ” aceasta produce performanИӣa medie O(n/m).

question.en: In the Rabin-Karp algorithm, what is a rolling hash?
question.ro: ГҺn algoritmul Rabin-Karp, ce este un rolling hash?

text.en: A hash function that uses a circular buffer
text.ro: O funcИӣie hash care foloseИҷte un buffer circular

text.en: A technique to compute the next window's hash from the previous one in O(1)
text.ro: O tehnicДғ de a calcula hash-ul ferestrei urmДғtoare din cel anterior Г®n O(1)

text.en: A hash function that never produces collisions
text.ro: O funcИӣie hash care nu produce niciodatДғ coliziuni

text.en: A method to hash 2D matrices
text.ro: O metodДғ de a calcula hash-ul matricelor 2D

explanation.en: The rolling hash updates the hash incrementally: remove the leftmost character's contribution, shift by multiplying by |ОЈ|, and add the new rightmost character. This gives O(1) per window instead of O(m), which is essential for Rabin-Karp's O(n+m) average performance.
explanation.ro: Rolling hash-ul actualizeazДғ hash-ul incremental: eliminДғ contribuИӣia caracterului din stГўnga, deplaseazДғ prin Г®nmulИӣire cu |ОЈ| Иҷi adaugДғ noul caracter din dreapta. Aceasta dДғ O(1) per fereastrДғ Г®n loc de O(m), esenИӣial pentru performanИӣa medie O(n+m) a Rabin-Karp.

question.en: What is a false positive in the context of Rabin-Karp?
question.ro: Ce este un false positive Г®n contextul Rabin-Karp?

text.en: A position where the pattern matches but the algorithm misses it
text.ro: O poziИӣie unde pattern-ul se potriveИҷte dar algoritmul o rateazДғ

text.en: A position where hashes match but the actual strings differ
text.ro: O poziИӣie unde hash-urile se potrivesc dar Иҷirurile reale diferДғ

text.en: An error in the rolling hash computation
text.ro: O eroare Г®n calculul rolling hash-ului

text.en: A hash value that overflows the integer limit
text.ro: O valoare hash care depДғИҷeИҷte limita Г®ntregului

explanation.en: Since Rabin-Karp works modulo a prime q, different strings can produce the same hash value (collision). When hashes match, the algorithm must verify character by character. A false positive is a collision where hash(text window) == hash(pattern) but the strings are actually different.
explanation.ro: Deoarece Rabin-Karp lucreazДғ modulo un prim q, Иҷiruri diferite pot produce aceeaИҷi valoare hash (coliziune). CГўnd hash-urile se potrivesc, algoritmul trebuie sДғ verifice caracter cu caracter. Un false positive este o coliziune unde hash(fereastra text) == hash(pattern) dar Иҷirurile sunt de fapt diferite.

question.en: Which algorithm performs best on average for large alphabets (e.g., Unicode text)?
question.ro: Care algoritm performeazДғ cel mai bine Г®n medie pentru alfabete mari (de ex., text Unicode)?

text.en: Naive вҖ” it has no preprocessing overhead
text.ro: Naiv вҖ” nu are overhead de preprocesare

text.en: KMP вҖ” it guarantees O(n) in all cases
text.ro: KMP вҖ” garanteazДғ O(n) Г®n toate cazurile

text.en: Boyer-Moore вҖ” large alphabets increase the effectiveness of the bad character rule
text.ro: Boyer-Moore вҖ” alfabetele mari cresc eficienИӣa regulii caracterului rДғu

text.en: Rabin-Karp вҖ” hashing is independent of alphabet size
text.ro: Rabin-Karp вҖ” hashing-ul este independent de dimensiunea alfabetului

explanation.en: With large alphabets, mismatches are more frequent and the bad character is more likely absent from the pattern entirely. This triggers the maximum shift (k+1), pushing Boyer-Moore's average case closer to O(n/m) вҖ” sublinear performance that no other algorithm achieves.
explanation.ro: Cu alfabete mari, mismatch-urile sunt mai frecvente Иҷi caracterul rДғu este mai probabil absent complet din pattern. Aceasta declanИҷeazДғ deplasarea maximДғ (k+1), Г®mpingГўnd cazul mediu al Boyer-Moore mai aproape de O(n/m) вҖ” performanИӣДғ subliniarДғ pe care niciun alt algoritm nu o atinge.

question.en: What unique capability does Rabin-Karp have that KMP and Boyer-Moore lack?
question.ro: Ce capacitate unicДғ are Rabin-Karp pe care KMP Иҷi Boyer-Moore nu o au?

text.en: It can match multiple patterns simultaneously
text.ro: Poate potrivi mai multe pattern-uri simultan

text.en: It guarantees no false positives
text.ro: GaranteazДғ absenИӣa false positives

text.en: It can be generalized to 2D pattern matching (matrices)
text.ro: Poate fi generalizat la potrivirea 2D a pattern-urilor (matrice)

text.en: It works without any preprocessing
text.ro: FuncИӣioneazДғ fДғrДғ nicio preprocesare

explanation.en: Rabin-Karp's hash-based approach naturally extends to 2D: compute row hashes for the pattern matrix, then use rolling hashes across columns of the text matrix. KMP and Boyer-Moore rely on sequential character comparison that doesn't generalize to 2D.
explanation.ro: Abordarea bazatДғ pe hash a Rabin-Karp se extinde natural la 2D: calculeazДғ hash-uri pe rГўnduri pentru matricea pattern, apoi foloseИҷte rolling hash-uri pe coloanele matricei text. KMP Иҷi Boyer-Moore se bazeazДғ pe comparare secvenИӣialДғ de caractere care nu se generalizeazДғ la 2D.

question.en: In Boyer-Moore, when does the bad character rule give a negative shift?
question.ro: ГҺn Boyer-Moore, cГўnd dДғ regula caracterului rДғu o deplasare negativДғ?

text.en: When the bad character is not in the pattern at all
text.ro: CГўnd caracterul rДғu nu este deloc Г®n pattern

text.en: When the rightmost occurrence of the bad character is to the right of the mismatch position
text.ro: CГўnd cea mai din dreapta apariИӣie a caracterului rДғu este la dreapta poziИӣiei de mismatch

text.en: When the pattern is longer than the remaining text
text.ro: CГўnd pattern-ul este mai lung decГўt textul rДғmas

text.en: When both shift rules give zero
text.ro: CГўnd ambele reguli de deplasare dau zero

explanation.en: The bad character shift is k - lastOcc[T[i+k]]. If lastOcc > k (the rightmost occurrence is to the right of the mismatch), the result is negative. In this case, we use shift = 1 or rely on the good suffix rule to provide a valid positive shift.
explanation.ro: Deplasarea caracterului rДғu este k - lastOcc[T[i+k]]. DacДғ lastOcc > k (cea mai din dreapta apariИӣie este la dreapta mismatch-ului), rezultatul este negativ. ГҺn acest caz, folosim deplasare = 1 sau ne bazДғm pe regula sufixului bun pentru a furniza o deplasare pozitivДғ validДғ.

question.en: What is the average-case complexity of Boyer-Moore?
question.ro: Care este complexitatea medie a algoritmului Boyer-Moore?

text.en: O(n)
text.ro: O(n)

text.en: O(nВ·m)
text.ro: O(nВ·m)

text.en: O(n/m) вҖ” sublinear
text.ro: O(n/m) вҖ” subliniar

text.en: O(n + m)
text.ro: O(n + m)

explanation.en: Boyer-Moore achieves O(n/m) on average вҖ” it can actually examine fewer characters than the length of the text! This sublinear behavior occurs because the bad character and good suffix rules allow the algorithm to skip large chunks of text without examining them. This is a key concept that often appears in algorithm analysis and exam questions.
explanation.ro: Boyer-Moore atinge O(n/m) Г®n medie вҖ” poate examina mai puИӣine caractere decГўt lungimea textului! Acest comportament subliniar apare deoarece regulile caracterului rДғu Иҷi sufixului bun permit algoritmului sДғ sarДғ porИӣiuni mari de text fДғrДғ a le examina. Acesta este un concept cheie care apare frecvent Г®n analiza algoritmilor Иҷi la examene.
